subject: Gaming
title: Minecraft: The Infinite Sandbox
description: A fast breakdown of Minecraft: The Infinite Sandbox for builders.

hook.text: A game with no story, no goals, and ugly graphics became the best-selling game of all time. How?

buildup.text: Markus Persson built Minecraft as a hobby project in 2009. Players could build anything from simple blocks.

discovery.text: Minecraft tapped into pure creativity. No rules, no limits. Players built cities, computers, even art.

twist.text: Microsoft bought Minecraft for $2.5 billion in 2014. A hobby project became worth more than most studios.

climax.text: Over 300 million copies sold. Minecraft proved that player freedom is more powerful than scripted spectacle.

punchline.text: No story. No goals. Ugly blocks. Best-selling game ever made.

quiz.question: How much did Microsoft pay for Minecraft?
